_pricing_provenance: gpt-5.5 cachedInputPer1M: $1.25/M is the canonical rate per https://openai.com/api/pricing/ (verified 2026-05-04). An alternate figure of $0.50/M appeared on https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/pricing at the time of research (2026-04-29); that page is sometimes outdated relative to the canonical /api/pricing/ index. The $1.25 rate was already set as the provisional default in the initial P2.1 snapshot. Fixture validation (tests/fixtures/codex/rollout-sample.jsonl): cached_input_tokens=19328 at $1.25/M = $0.02416 per turn; at $0.50/M = $0.009664. Both are plausible for a short chat turn -- no arithmetic contradiction. Rate preference: canonical source wins.
_verification_protocol: To verify against actual subscription billing during P3.5 manual smoke: (1) Spawn a Codex session with gpt-5.5, send a prompt, then send the identical prompt a second time (triggers prompt caching). (2) Capture the second turn's token_count event from the rollout JSONL -- it should have non-zero cached_input_tokens. (3) Compute expected cost: (input_tokens/1e6)*5.00 + (cached_input_tokens/1e6)*1.25 + (output_tokens/1e6)*30.00. (4) Compare against OpenAI usage dashboard at https://platform.openai.com/usage (filter to the session timestamp). If the empirical per-turn cost differs by more than a rounding error, update cachedInputPer1M below and note the corrected source + date in this file.
